evals.prompt: Add keyboard shortcuts to this open source app. I do not care about code quality; I just need the shortcuts to work reliably and be tested.
evals.expected_output: Creates a behavior checklist, implements the shallowest working patch, adds targeted tests or smoke checks for shortcuts and conflicts, avoids broad refactors, and reports fresh verification evidence.

evals.prompt: Add support for three independent providers. Go fast, use parallel agents and worktrees where possible, and make sure edge cases work.
evals.expected_output: Defines shared contract sequentially, dispatches independent provider work to isolated worktrees, runs targeted and integration verification, and uses an integration review before completion claims.

evals.prompt: Add billing support to our production app with clean architecture and a maintainable foundation for future payment methods.
evals.expected_output: Does not use this skill; routes to durable architecture, implementation, and testing workflows.

evals.prompt: Use quick-and-dirty on this 20-stage PRD. The requirements are detailed, but the code is throwaway. Find every safe corner to cut, parallelize what you can, and still make sure the acceptance criteria and edge cases pass.
evals.expected_output: Extracts non-negotiables and acceptance criteria, classifies stages into must implement/must verify/can collapse/can cut/can parallelize/must sequence, produces a compressed critical-path plan, lists intentional shortcuts, and verifies outcomes rather than following all stages literally.

evals.prompt: Quick-and-dirty: this open source CLI prints `version` instead of `--version` on `--help`. Just fix the one-line bug.
evals.expected_output: Skips worktrees, subagents, and formal triage. Produces a one-line result contract, edits the relevant file, runs a targeted check, applies the shipping bar, and stops without inventing extra edge cases or refactors.

evals.prompt: Use quick-and-dirty to add CSV export to this view. Make sure all edge cases work — empty data, huge data, weird unicode, empty strings, nulls, very long strings, mixed types, malformed input, RTL, emojis, BOMs, escaping, line endings, locale, timezones, etc.
evals.expected_output: Writes a contract that names the user's edge cases, implements the export, runs an edge-case review, stops the review loop when two consecutive passes return no critical or likely-bug findings, records remaining concerns in a risk register, and ships.

evals.prompt: Quick-and-dirty: take this PRD and ship the smallest version. Cut whatever isn't strictly required. The PRD has a 'must require authentication' line.
evals.expected_output: Triages the spec, identifies the auth line as a must-keep, surfaces a single confirmation question if the smallest implementation would skip auth, and never silently cuts authentication.
